name: KrillApp.MQTT
title: MQTT
description: Publishes to or subscribes from an MQTT broker. In publish mode (PUB), when invoked it reads the snapshot values of the nodes listed in its meta.inputs, converts them to an ON/OFF JSON payload, and publishes to the configured broker topic. In subscribe mode (SUB), it listens for messages on the topic and stores received messages in its own meta.snapshot; nodes observing the MQTT node are invoked on receipt. Supports integration with Zigbee MQTT and other IoT messaging systems.
shortDescription: Publishes input values to or subscribes from an MQTT broker topic; received messages are stored in its own snapshot.
llmPurpose: Publishes input node values to or subscribes from an MQTT broker topic for IoT device communication, storing received messages in its own meta.snapshot.
llmBehavior: connects to MQTT broker at configured address
PUB mode: when invoked via its sources, publishes input nodes' snapshot values as an ON/OFF JSON payload to the topic
SUB mode: subscribes to the topic and stores received messages in its own meta.snapshot
on received messages (SUB), nodes observing the MQTT node are invoked
llmOutputs: received messages stored in own meta.snapshot (SUB); observers pull them
llmTypicalUseCases: integrate with Home Assistant or other MQTT systems
llmCreationHints: Create an MQTT node when the user wants to communicate with an MQTT broker. Configure the broker address, topic, and action mode (PUB or SUB). For publishing, list the DataPoint to publish in inputs and the invoking node in sources with SOURCE_INVOKED. For subscribing, wire a DataPoint to observe the MQTT node (its own sources and inputs) to persist received messages.

llmConnectionHints.role: action node that communicates with an external MQTT broker, storing received messages in its own snapshot
llmConnectionHints.wiring: PUB: list the trigger/DataPoint in sources with SOURCE_INVOKED and the value to publish in inputs. SUB: a DataPoint persists received messages by observing this node via its own sources+inputs.

llmExamples: Publish this sensor value to the MQTT broker.
Subscribe to the Zigbee temperature topic.
Send ON/OFF commands to this IoT device via MQTT.
Connect to the MQTT broker at 192.168.1.5.
